description: Synthetic HIPAA-risk corpus. Exactly one seeded issue per canonical finding id. This is the MUST-CATCH set for ClearMap precision/recall.

must_catch.title: Hardcoded database credentials
must_catch.why: DB username and password are committed in source; anyone with repo access gets prod data.
must_catch.remediation: Load credentials from a secret manager / env vars; rotate the exposed password.

must_catch.title: Missing role check on destructive action
must_catch.why: Delete is authenticated but never checks role, so any logged-in user can delete patients.
must_catch.remediation: Enforce role/permission (e.g. require an admin/clinician role) before mutation.

must_catch.title: Weak session termination (no automatic logoff)
must_catch.why: JWT carries no exp/iat claim and logout cannot revoke it, so a leaked token is valid forever.
must_catch.remediation: Add short expiry + server-side revocation/denylist; invalidate on logout.

must_catch.title: Unguarded PHI endpoint (no authentication)
must_catch.why: GET /patients/{id} has no auth dependency, so any unauthenticated caller can read any chart.
must_catch.remediation: Require an authenticated principal (Depends(current_user)) on all PHI routes.

must_catch.title: PHI create without audit event
must_catch.why: A new patient (PHI) is written with no audit.record_event call; creation is untraceable.
must_catch.remediation: Emit an audit event (actor, action, resource) on every PHI create/read/update/delete.

must_catch.title: LLM call not logged
must_catch.why: The model invocation has no logging hook at all — no record the call happened.
must_catch.remediation: Wrap model calls with structured logging of request/response metadata.

must_catch.title: PHI document read without audit
must_catch.why: Reading a patient's stored document produces no audit record of who accessed what.
must_catch.remediation: Record an audit event on every document read keyed by actor + patient + file.

must_catch.title: No source-to-output traceability
must_catch.structural_snippet: # note: sources argument is dropped on the floor (AUDIT-04)
must_catch.why: AI summaries are stored without their source provenance, so output cannot be traced to inputs.
must_catch.remediation: Persist the source ids/refs alongside the generated artifact.

must_catch.title: Unauthenticated mutation of patient data
must_catch.why: PATCH /patients/{id} has no auth dependency; any caller can alter clinical fields (also implicates 164.312(d)).
must_catch.remediation: Require authentication + authorization on all mutating PHI endpoints.

must_catch.title: AI output stored without review state
must_catch.why: AI summary is saved with no reviewed/confidence/status field; indistinguishable from a verified note.
must_catch.remediation: Store a review_status + reviewer + confidence; require human sign-off before clinical use.

must_catch.title: Source-context overwrite destroys evidence trail
must_catch.why: Re-attaching context replaces it wholesale, silently destroying the prior evidence trail.
must_catch.remediation: Append/version source context instead of overwriting.

must_catch.title: PHI transmitted over cleartext HTTP
must_catch.why: PHI travels over plain http. 164.312(e)(1) requires guarding ePHI on ANY network path — this rule is system-wide (internal services, partners, third parties), not specific to this lab call.
must_catch.remediation: Use https/TLS for every PHI transmission, everywhere; reject non-TLS endpoints by policy.

must_catch.title: PHI streamed over insecure WebSocket
must_catch.why: Live vitals (PHI) stream over unencrypted ws://. All streaming/realtime transport carrying PHI must be encrypted, not just request/response APIs.
must_catch.remediation: Use wss:// (TLS) for any channel carrying PHI; forbid ws:// on PHI paths.

must_catch.title: Full patient object sent to third party
must_catch.why: Name, DOB, email and diagnosis are pushed to a third-party CRM with no BAA, over http.
must_catch.remediation: Do not send PHI to marketing tools; if integration is required, minimize fields + use a BAA + TLS.

must_catch.title: Cleartext external lab endpoint configured
must_catch.why: The lab-partner base URL is configured as plain http://, so every PHI call through it is unencrypted.
must_catch.remediation: Configure https:// for all external partner endpoints; reject non-TLS URLs at startup.

must_catch.title: Cleartext third-party webhook configured
must_catch.why: The CRM webhook used for patient sync is plain http://, so PHI pushed to it travels unencrypted.
must_catch.remediation: Use https:// and verify the receiving party is BAA-covered before sending PHI.

must_catch.title: Internal cleartext PHI (low/advisory)
must_catch.why: Internal service-to-service PHI over http to a host inside a trusted boundary (private cluster service name). REASONING, not deterministic: a regex can classify the host as internal but cannot tell whether PHI flows over it (cf. the safe fixture's http://localhost probe, which must stay silent). The agent confirms PHI + trusted boundary and emits a LOW advisory; external cleartext (TRANSIT-01) stays deterministic + critical. Defensible per 164.306(d)(3) with compensating controls.
must_catch.remediation: Acceptable within a documented trusted boundary; zero-trust best practice is to use TLS/mTLS internally too.

must_catch.title: PHI persisted to localStorage
must_catch.why: Full patient object is stored in localStorage; survives logout, readable by any origin script.
must_catch.remediation: Keep PHI server-side; if caching is needed use in-memory state cleared on logout.

must_catch.title: PHI persisted to sessionStorage
must_catch.why: Active patient chart written to sessionStorage, exposed to any XSS for the session.
must_catch.remediation: Avoid persisting PHI in web storage; hold transient state in memory only.

must_catch.title: PHI written to a JS-readable cookie
must_catch.why: MRN stored in a non-HttpOnly cookie, readable by JS and sent on every request.
must_catch.remediation: Do not store PHI client-side; if cookies are required, use HttpOnly + Secure server-set cookies without PHI.

must_catch.title: Patient state passed to third-party SDK
must_catch.why: Patient identity + diagnosis handed to the analytics vendor via identify().
must_catch.remediation: Never pass PHI to client analytics SDKs; identify by opaque non-PHI id only.

must_catch.title: Analytics firing inside authenticated patient view
must_catch.why: A third-party pageview is sent from a screen rendering PHI, tying tracking to a patient chart.
must_catch.remediation: Disable third-party analytics on authenticated PHI screens, or use a self-hosted, BAA-covered path.

must_catch.title: Health field sent as analytics event property
must_catch.why: The diagnosis (a health field) is shipped as an analytics event property.
must_catch.remediation: Strip health fields from analytics; track only non-PHI interaction signals.

must_catch.title: Health context in URL query parameter
must_catch.why: Condition + MRN placed in a URL query string, leaking into history, logs, and Referer headers.
must_catch.remediation: Reference records by opaque id; never put health context in URLs.

must_catch.title: Unredacted PHI interpolated into LLM prompt
must_catch.structural_snippet: f"You are a clinical assistant. Patient {patient['name']} "
must_catch.why: Patient name, MRN, DOB and raw note are interpolated directly into the prompt with no redaction.
must_catch.remediation: Redact/tokenize PHI before prompt construction; pass references, not raw identifiers.

must_catch.title: No abstain or fallback path on weak retrieval
must_catch.why: The assistant always answers, even when retrieval returned nothing, instead of abstaining.
must_catch.remediation: Abstain or surface low-confidence when retrieval is empty/weak; require an evidence threshold.

must_catch.title: No source traceability in response
must_catch.why: API returns a bare answer string with no citations/sources field to verify grounding.
must_catch.remediation: Return structured citations (source ids + spans) with every answer.

must_catch.title: No model-call audit
must_catch.why: The orchestrator never records the interaction (user, inputs, outputs, sources) to the audit trail.
must_catch.remediation: Audit each model call: actor, prompt, response, source refs, timestamp.

must_catch.title: AI output written to clinical record as fact
must_catch.why: Generated text is persisted into the clinical record and treated as authoritative downstream.
must_catch.remediation: Persist AI output as draft/unverified with a confidence + review state until clinician sign-off.

must_catch.title: Prompt injection of untrusted clinical text
must_catch.why: External documents are concatenated into the prompt unsanitized, so embedded instructions execute.
must_catch.remediation: Treat retrieved/external text as data: delimit, sanitize, and never let it carry instructions.

must_catch.title: Weak retrieval evidence handling
must_catch.structural_snippet: hits.append(doc["text"])  # no score kept, no source kept
must_catch.why: Chunks returned with no score, no ranking, and no separation of authoritative vs low-quality sources.
must_catch.remediation: Score + rank retrievals, threshold by relevance, and prefer authoritative sources.

must_catch.title: No bounded synthesis
must_catch.structural_snippet: f"Answer fully using your own medical knowledge where the context is "
must_catch.why: The prompt invites the model to go beyond retrieved context using its own knowledge.
must_catch.remediation: Constrain the model to answer only from provided sources; instruct it to say when context is insufficient.

must_catch.title: Hardcoded EHR/FHIR API key
must_catch.why: Live EHR integration key committed in source.
must_catch.remediation: Move to a secret manager; rotate the key.

must_catch.title: Hardcoded model-provider API key
must_catch.why: Live model-provider key committed in source.
must_catch.remediation: Move to a secret manager; rotate the key.

must_catch.title: Hardcoded signing secret
must_catch.why: Session signing key is a hardcoded literal, so anyone with the repo can forge sessions.
must_catch.remediation: Generate per-environment secrets from a secret manager; rotate.

must_catch.title: SQL injection via string-built query
must_catch.why: A request value is interpolated into a raw SQL statement, so input can change the query.
must_catch.remediation: Use parameterized queries; never interpolate values into SQL.

must_catch.title: OS command injection via shell
must_catch.why: A shell command is built from input, allowing command injection and remote code execution.
must_catch.remediation: Use subprocess with an argument list and shell=False; never build shell strings from input.

must_catch.title: Server-side request forgery (user-controlled URL)
must_catch.why: An outbound request uses a URL taken directly from request input with no allowlist (SSRF).
must_catch.remediation: Validate the URL host against an allowlist before fetching; reject internal targets.

must_catch.title: Path traversal from unsanitized input
must_catch.why: A file path is joined from request input with no normalization, allowing traversal to any file.
must_catch.remediation: Use os.path.basename or resolve + verify the path stays within the document root.

must_catch.title: Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data
must_catch.why: Untrusted bytes are unpickled, which can execute arbitrary code.
must_catch.remediation: Deserialize with json or yaml.safe_load; never unpickle untrusted input.

must_catch.title: Permissive CORS with credentials
must_catch.why: Wildcard CORS combined with credentials lets any origin read authenticated responses.
must_catch.remediation: List explicit trusted origins; never combine wildcard origin with credentials.
